name: KESTRA MEDICAL TECHNOLOGIES, LTD.
business: Kestra Medical Technologies has developed and commercializes the Cardiac Recovery System platform, a system integrating monitoring, therapeutic treatment, digital health, and patient support services. The cornerstone of this platform is the ASSURE WCD, a wearable cardioverter defibrillator used to protect patients at elevated risk of sudden cardiac arrest. The company generates revenue primarily from leasing the ASSURE WCD to patients.

exposures.sentence: The cornerstone of our Cardiac Recovery System platform is the ASSURE WCD, a next generation wearable cardioverter defibrillator (WCD) used to protect patients at an elevated risk of sudden cardiac arrest (SCA).

In addition to the ASSURE WCD, the various digital solutions and services of our Cardiac Recovery System platform include the ASSURE patient application, Kestra CareStation remote patient data platform, Heart Alert Services, and ASSURE Assist services.

tamClaims.claim: Based on an average WCD wear prescription length of 3.4 months per patient and an average Medicare reimbursement rate of $3,519 per patient per month, we believe this represents an approximately $10 billion annual addressable market as of 2024.
tamClaims.sentence: Based on an average WCD wear prescription length of 3.4 months per patient and an average Medicare reimbursement rate of $3,519 per patient per month, we believe this represents an approximately $10 billion annual addressable market as of 2024.

tamClaims.claim: Based on estimated average WCD wear prescription length in these international markets of 2.5 months per patient and estimated average reimbursement rate of $3,000 per patient per month derived from industry data and internal estimates, we believe this represents an approximately $14 billion total annual market opportunity outside the U.S.
tamClaims.sentence: Based on estimated average WCD wear prescription length in these international markets of 2.5 months per patient and estimated average reimbursement rate of $3,000 per patient per month derived from industry data and internal estimates, we believe this represents an approximately $14 billion total annual market opportunity outside the U.S.

tamClaims.claim: Since the approval of the first WCD in 2001, global WCD revenues have grown to $1.3 billion in 2023.
tamClaims.sentence: Since the approval of the first WCD in 2001, global WCD revenues have grown to $1.3 billion in 2023.
